findings.evidence: `nl -ba code/TauCeti/Algebra/Group/NormalizerQuotientConjugation.lean` shows `@[simp]` on `normalizerQuotientEquivMap_refl_mk` at line 148 and on `normalizerQuotientEquivMap_trans_mk_congr` at line 183, but no attribute before `normalizerQuotientEquivMap_refl_mk_congr` at line 170.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to `normalizerQuotientEquivMap_refl_mk_congr`, matching the deck specialization and the composite congruence lemma.
findings.issue: `normalizerQuotientEquivMap_refl_mk_congr` is the characteristic normal form saying identity transport, after the canonical subgroup congruence, fixes representatives, but it lacks `@[simp]`; the existing simp lemma at line 149 rewrites only to the `H.map id` target.

summary: The deck-level composition annotation is fixed, but the generic identity normal form is still not exposed to simplification. This leaves the public `normalizerQuotientEquivMap` API stopping at the `H.map id` quotient unless users manually rewrite the congruence lemma.
